DIVERGE outcome: Fault vs Completed

Status: outcome-class mismatch (CellGov reaches a fault at step
100,047; RPCS3 captured at first sys_tty_write completes the
checkpoint hook normally). The comparator stops at the outcome
discriminant before reading regions, so no per-region byte report
is produced from this pair.

Boot trajectory: post-Phase-29 (worker-thread callback-dispatch
primitive + cellSaveDataAutoLoad real funcStat callback), CellGov
runs flOw end-to-end through the title's `MODE_AUTO_LOAD`
sequence: funcStat fires, the title's `cb_data_status_load` dumps
StatGet, prints "Data NPUA80001-FLOW- is not found", takes the
fresh-install branch, returns from `cellSaveDataAutoLoad` with
CELL_SAVEDATA_ERROR_NODATA (0x8002b40b), `thr_auto_load()` ends,
and the title enters
`CFlOwApplication::m_InitEntityHierarchy`. A NULL-bcctr-into-vtable
fault then surfaces at step 100,047 with LR=0x000923e8 (the
generic vtable indirect-call helper at 0x000923c0). r1 sits in a
child-stack region and r2=0, so the fault is in a child PPU
thread spawned by entity-init code, not the main thread.

The previous frontier (pre-Phase-29) was step 86,527 with the
same NULL-bcctr shape but in the post-AutoLoad return path
because callbacks never fired. Phase 29 unblocked that surface;
the new fault opens a Stage C investigation against an
uninitialized vtable populated by some yet-to-implement HLE
primitive (most likely a sysmodule whose `module_start` was
supposed to install the slot via cellSysmoduleLoadModule).

The RPCS3 baseline (rpcs3.dump) is unchanged: it captures at the
first sys_tty_write under headless mode and is not affected by
CellGov-side changes.

Verdict: not currently comparable at the captured checkpoint.
CellGov terminates in a fault before reaching a first
sys_tty_write that produces a memory snapshot RPCS3 also reaches.
A shared-checkpoint mechanism (e.g., CellGov-side "stop at Nth
sys_tty_write" flag mirroring CELLGOV_DUMP_TTY_NTH) is the
prerequisite for the next refresh; once that lands, the byte-level
compare can run at the same logical execution point.

Re-confirmed at the current branch tip: outcome is FAULT at step
100,047, deterministic across runs (bit-identical step count and
fault PC).
